works.name: Ho System
works.short_description: Published open methodology for human-AI collaborative software development in which the human practitioner maintains full architectural authority while using AI as an implementation partner. The core unit is a ho — a bounded ~2-hour session producing something real, connecting to the project arc via commit hash, ending with structured reflection. Practitioners progress through Shu-Ha-Ri stages; tiered understanding (Black Box, Functional, Deep) gives an honest frame for tracking what's understood versus delegated.
substantive_description: The dominant frame for AI-assisted development assumes the model leads. Vibe coding names the position with unembarrassed clarity: the human gestures, the model generates, the human accepts or rejects. The Ho System refuses the position.

It treats AI as an implementation partner inside a discipline the human owns — architectural authority held, structural decisions made consciously, every session bounded, every artifact reviewed. Eight production systems shipped under it, including a computer vision platform at Harvard and a ZFS backup orchestrator with 278 tests at 99% coverage.

The deeper claim is about agency in the age of capable models. Capable models tempt the practitioner to delegate the parts of the work that build the practitioner's judgment. The Ho System is the operational answer to that temptation: a methodology that lets you ship without becoming someone who cannot ship alone.

state.last: **ho-12 OPENED** (2026-07-11, the ho-status companion). Authored the ho + 5 agent-task specs (private). Design settled with the practitioner: a *derived* progress roster (not a fifth hand-maintained source) — one source (ho frontmatter `title`/`description`/`status` + K4 phase order) → three renders (a `ho-status.json` feed, a `ho-status.md` glance with a single `state` enum → Doc×Done glyphs, and a cross-project HTML dashboard, ho-13). Root `metadata/` zone holds `keisaku.json` + `ho-status.json` + `ho-status.md`; visibility inherited from the repo's ho-process posture (gitignore `metadata/` iff ho work is private); the dashboard reads local working trees. keisaku stays a co-located light cache, coordinated via a handoff prompt (relocate + summary passthrough). **Two framework commits landed** (branch `ho-12-ho-status-companion`): `654d9b2` — `title`/`description` required practitioner-track frontmatter (ho-structure §5.1, design 3.9, K5 skill repo + installed); `249df89` (AT-02) — the ho-status doctrine (kamae-project-framing §2.4 with json schema + `state` enum + md render + visibility posture; `metadata/` zone §2.6; refresh in cross-session-continuity §3; glossary; CHANGELOG). **AT-04 done** (local): keisaku handoff prompt at `ho-process/agent-tasks/keisaku-metadata-handoff.md`. Prior arc cold-banked: ho-10/11 closed, 2.14 shipped, K6 fleet 20/20, keisaku staged.
state.next: Two tasks remain on ho-12: **AT-03** (skill wiring — K5 close gains roster-regen in SKILL.md + the four shape closes; K4 overview notes the companion; scaffolder seeds root `metadata/` + gitignores it when ho-work is private; sync all installed `~/.claude` copies, ho-11 insertion precedent), then **AT-05** (backfill title/description onto the 13 hos + produce ho-system's `metadata/ho-status.{json,md}` + add `metadata/` to `.gitignore`). Then close ho-12, add its K4 entry, PR the branch. ho-13 = generator + dashboard; ho-14 = fleet rollout (Sonnet titles, Haiku generation).
